other_observers: Brandon Percival, John Drummond, Jeannie Mitchell, Aaron Shipe and one more person.; I saw the bird first and yelled cuckoo. I think we all went through the I.D. visual clues together.
specific_location: Just south of the Chico Basin Ranch Headquarters in a known migrant trap there.
description: I saw this bird first in flight coming OUT OF A MARSH. In flight it had the easily recongizable shape of a cuckoo. I yelled "cuckoo" to the group I was birding with.   when perched I looked for an saw the bill was two-toned, dark at the distal 1/2 and gray at the base. It appeared to be thin. I looked at the orbit and it appeared to be greenish and narrow. The eye was dark. The tail was very long. The wings were brown with hints of rufous and there were buff edges on the wing coverts. In flight I looked for rufous wing patches and did not see any.Behaviors: First flying toward a grove of trees and then landing and then flying back to another tree grove over a 60 second time period.Call: none heardPlumage: 1st year
